goal: Connect the floor to GameManager so the vertical slice is playable: spawn, objectives, win/lose.
summary: Phase 5 wired the F1_Basement floor to GameManager: player spawn marker resolution, RegistrationBoard + SequenceManager placement, staircase win trigger, and Listener spawn/patrol placement. All steps verified via grep counts and a clean dotnet build.
stepSummaries:
1. Update GameManager.PlayerSpawnPosition to use the F1_Basement player spawn marker coordinate (or add a NodePath export if safer). [verified]
  2. Place RegistrationBoard and wire it to the SequenceManager path in GameManager. [verified]
  3. Add a staircase Area3D that, on body entered after SequenceManager.IsComplete, calls GameManager.OnFinalBroadcastTransmitted or directly emits Victory. [verified]
  4. Verify Listener spawn and patrol: place Listener.tscn at ListenerSpawn marker and confirm it cycles waypoints. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Load-bearing steps used real verification: dotnet build and grep counts. Non-load-bearing layout checks were proxy/syntactic but the compile and node-presence checks cover the functional wiring.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Steps 1-4 each ended with dotnet build LastWord.sln (0 errors) and grep counts for the relevant nodes/paths.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Combined output makes the floor playable: GameManager resolves player spawn from F1_Basement marker, RegistrationBoard and SequenceManager exist in the scene with GameManager paths set, staircase Area3D triggers win, and Listener is placed at the spawn marker with waypoint loop available.
gateVerdicts.evidence: GameScene.tscn sets PlayerSpawnMarkerPath and SequenceManagerPath; F1_Basement.tscn contains RegistrationBoard, SequenceManager, Staircase_WinZone, Listener, and 16 Waypoints.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in this phase; all four steps completed.
